natural_language_statement: For Nesterov's first accelerated proximal gradient scheme on min f(x) + h(x) with f convex L-smooth and h closed convex, given step sizes t_k and momentum factors γ_k satisfying (1 - γ_k) t_k / γ_k^2 ≤ t_{k-1} / γ_{k-1}^2 (with γ_0 = 1, t_k ∈ (0, 1/L]), the iterates x_k satisfy (f + h)(x_{k+1}) - (f + h)(xm) ≤ γ_k^2 / (2 t_k) · ‖x_0 - xm‖^2. With γ_k ≈ 2/(k+2), this gives the O(1/k^2) acceleration.
assumptions: E is a complete real inner product space
f, h : E → ℝ convex; f L-smooth (gradient L-Lipschitz)
Iterates: y_k = x_k + (γ_k (1 - γ_{k-1}) / γ_{k-1}) (x_k - x_{k-1}) for k ≥ 1; x_{k+1} = prox_{t_k h}(y_k - t_k f'(y_k))
Initial conditions: y_0 = x_0, γ_0 = 1, x_0 fixed
Step/momentum constraints: 0 < t_k ≤ 1/L; 0 < γ_k ≤ 1; (1 - γ_k) t_k / γ_k^2 ≤ t_{k-1} / γ_{k-1}^2 for k ≥ 1
conclusion: ∀ k, f (alg.x (k + 1)) + h (alg.x (k + 1)) - f xm - h xm ≤ (alg.γ k) ^ 2 / (2 * alg.t k) * ‖x0 - xm‖ ^ 2
proof_sketch: Subgradient characterization of prox: y_k - x_{k+1} - t_k f'(y_k) ∈ t_k ∂h(x_{k+1}); so for any z, t_k h(x_{k+1}) ≤ t_k h(z) + ⟨f'(y_k) + (1/t_k)(x_{k+1} - y_k), z - x_{k+1}⟩ · t_k  (after rearranging).
Descent lemma on f at y_k, x_{k+1}: f(x_{k+1}) ≤ f(y_k) + ⟨f'(y_k), x_{k+1} - y_k⟩ + (1/(2t_k)) ‖x_{k+1} - y_k‖^2 (using t_k ≤ 1/L).
Convexity of f at y_k: f(y_k) + ⟨f'(y_k), z - y_k⟩ ≤ f(z).
Combine to get for any z: φ(x_{k+1}) ≤ φ(z) + (1/t_k)⟨x_{k+1} - y_k, z - x_{k+1}⟩ + (1/(2t_k)) ‖x_{k+1} - y_k‖^2.

Take a convex combination of z = x_k (weight 1 - γ_k) and z = xm (weight γ_k): φ(x_{k+1}) ≤ (1 - γ_k) φ(x_k) + γ_k φ(xm) + (1/(2t_k))(‖z_k - xm‖^2 - ‖z_{k+1} - xm‖^2) · γ_k^2 where z_k is an extrapolated point.
Define Lyapunov V_k = (t_{k-1}/γ_{k-1}^2)(φ(x_k) - φ(xm)) + (1/2)‖z_k - xm‖^2 (with V_0 = (1/2)‖x_0 - xm‖^2). The momentum/step constraint cond gives V_{k+1} ≤ V_k.
Therefore (t_k / γ_k^2)(φ(x_{k+1}) - φ(xm)) ≤ V_{k+1} ≤ V_0 = (1/2)‖x_0 - xm‖^2; rearrange.
